{{Short description="SI base unit of length}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Metre
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[International System of Units]], [[Speed of light]], [[Length]]
| unit_system = [[SI base unit]]
| unit_of = [[Length]]
| symbol = m
}}

The '''metre''' (symbol: '''m''') is the [[SI base unit]] of [[length]]. Since 1983, it has been defined as the length of the path travelled by [[light]] in vacuum during a time interval of 1/299,792,458 of a [[second]]:

:<math>1\ \text{m} = c \times \frac{1}{299,792,458}\ \text{s}</math>

where <math>c = 299,792,458\ \text{m/s}</math> is the exact defined value of the [[speed of light]].

== Historical definitions ==
* 1793 — one ten-millionth of the distance from the [[North Pole]] to the [[equator]] along the [[Paris meridian]]
* 1799 — ''Mètre des Archives'', a platinum bar
* 1889 — International Prototype Metre, a platinum–iridium bar
* 1960 — 1,650,763.73 wavelengths of the orange-red line of [[krypton-86]]
* 1983 — defined via the speed of light (current definition)

== Multiples and conversions ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Submultiples !! Multiples
|-
| millimetre (mm) = 10⁻³ m || kilometre (km) = 10³ m
|-
| micrometre (μm) = 10⁻⁶ m || megametre (Mm) = 10⁶ m
|-
| nanometre (nm) = 10⁻⁹ m || gigametre (Gm) = 10⁹ m
|}

* 1 metre ≈ 3.28084 [[foot (unit)|feet]]
* 1 metre ≈ 39.3701 [[inch]]es

== Metre in physics equations ==
* [[Speed]]: <math>v = d/t</math> → measured in m/s
* [[Acceleration]]: <math>a = \Delta v/\Delta t</math> → m/s²
* [[Force]]: <math>F = ma</math> → kg·m/s² = N
* [[Energy]]: <math>E = F\cdot d</math> → kg·m²/s² = J

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[International System of Units]]
* [[Kilogram]]
* [[Second]]
* [[Speed of light]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Metre| ]]
[[Category:SI base units]]
[[Category:Units of length]]
